external_memory_interfaces.spec: 16-bit × 4-channel data bus, up to 8 GB total

packages: FC-BGA, 17 mm × 17 mm × 2.452 mm, 0.65 mm pitch, 625 balls (25 × 25)
notes: Datasheet: 45 pages, DEEPX commercial datasheet v1.0, June 2025 (DEEPX Confidential).

DX-M1 is a *companion accelerator*, not a host SoC — it sits behind a PCIe Gen3 ×4 interface attached to a host CPU/MPU.  On ALP Lab's V2N-M1 SoMs the host is the Renesas RZ/V2N (R9A09G056N44GBG) and PCIe is wired internally on the SoM.
Optimised for INT8 CNN inference (ResNet, MobileNet, YOLO and similar topologies).  25 TOPS dense throughput @ 1.0 GHz.
Internal Cortex-M55 runs DEEPX firmware that handles model load, scheduling, and PCIe doorbell.  Application code does not address it directly.
Per-channel LPDDR capacity is 2 GB; up to 4 channels = 8 GB max model + activation memory on the accelerator side.
PVT sensor exposes integrated temperature + voltage monitoring via on-chip registers.
Security: TRNG, cryptographic accelerator, and chip-lifecycle management on-die — see datasheet §8.5.

Toolchain: DEEPX SDK (model compiler + runtime).  The ALP SDK exposes `<alp/iot.h>` / `<alp/peripheral.h>` for host-side interaction; DX-M1-specific APIs land in v0.3 alongside the DEEPX SDK integration.
